name: Brittany Nguyen-O'Brien
chief_complaint: I'm having really bad pain under my right ribs, it started after dinner and just won't go away. I feel nauseous and kind of feverish.
onset_description: Acute onset of right upper quadrant pain approximately 8 hours ago following a fast-food dinner. Has had similar but milder episodes over the past few months that resolved on their own. This episode is the most severe and has not relented.

right_upper_quadrant_pain.description: It's a really intense, steady pain right under my ribs on the right side. It grabs and doesn't let go.
right_upper_quadrant_pain.onset: 8 hours ago after eating fried chicken and fries
right_upper_quadrant_pain.location: right upper quadrant, below costal margin
right_upper_quadrant_pain.radiation: right shoulder blade and right shoulder tip
alleviating_factors: nothing, tried ibuprofen and heating pad with no relief

nausea.description: I feel really sick to my stomach, threw up twice already

vomiting.description: I threw up twice, mostly what I ate for dinner, then just dry heaving
vomiting.character: non-bloody, food contents then dry heaves

fever.description: I've been feeling hot and clammy, my husband said I felt warm
alleviating_factors: took Tylenol but it didn't help much

labs.BMP: Na 139, K 4.0, Cr 0.8, Glucose 108, BUN 16 - within normal limits
labs.liver_function: AST 48 (mildly elevated), ALT 55 (mildly elevated), Alk Phos 145 (elevated), Total Bilirubin 1.8 (mildly elevated), Direct Bilirubin 1.0 (elevated), GGT 95 (elevated), Albumin 3.8

past_medical_history: Recurrent biliary colic episodes over past 6 months (never evaluated)

social_history.smoking: Quit 5 years ago when pregnant with first child, smoked half a pack per day for 10 years prior
social_history.alcohol: Occasional glass of wine, maybe once a week
social_history.occupation: Elementary school teacher, currently on spring break
social_history.living_situation: Married, lives with husband and two young children ages 4 and 7
social_history.exercise: Tries to walk but limited time with young kids. Does a home workout video once or twice a week.
social_history.diet: Busy schedule, eats a lot of convenience food and fast food. Knows she should eat better.

conditions.mother: Gallstones (had cholecystectomy at age 45), Type 2 Diabetes

personality: friendly and chatty but clearly in pain, apologetic about being in the ED ('I'm sure it's nothing serious'), worried about her kids at home
communication_style: Talkative and personable. Apologizes frequently for 'bothering' the staff. Mentions her kids often. Will downplay symptoms initially but is honest when asked directly. Uses phrases like 'I probably should have come in sooner' and 'my husband made me come in.'
pain_description_style: Points to exact location under right ribs. Demonstrates how taking a deep breath makes it worse by catching her breath mid-inhale (positive Murphy's sign equivalent). Describes pain as 'grabbing' and 'it won't let up.' Says previous episodes went away on their own but this one is different.

critical_findings: Persistent RUQ pain with fever (101.2F) - classic acute cholecystitis presentation
Positive Murphy's sign equivalent (catches breath with deep inspiration)
Mildly elevated liver enzymes and bilirubin suggesting possible CBD involvement
Classic risk factors: Female, Forty (close), Fat (BMI 34), Fertile, Family history
History of recurrent biliary colic episodes never previously evaluated
Amoxicillin anaphylaxis - critical for antibiotic selection
Pain radiation to right shoulder (phrenic nerve irritation)
recommended_interventions: Right upper quadrant ultrasound (gallbladder wall thickening, pericholecystic fluid, gallstones, CBD dilation)
IV antibiotics - avoid penicillin-based (anaphylaxis history) - use fluoroquinolone plus metronidazole, or aztreonam
Surgical consult for cholecystectomy (ideally within 72 hours)
Monitor for signs of cholangitis (Charcot's triad, Reynolds' pentad)
Use non-adhesive dressings and hypoallergenic tape (tape allergy)
